description: Fix 3 failing stress tests: 2 in stress-network-auth.e2e.ts (audit log + secret rotation) and 1 in stress-adapter-injection.e2e.ts (adapter file missing recovery). Root causes: tool calls before extension is ready, and test setup issues.

userStories.title: Fix audit log stress test — tools called before extension ready
userStories.description: The '50 sequential tool calls' test fails because echo calls are made before the extension has connected and a matching tab is open. Fix by ensuring the dispatch pipeline is ready before starting the 50-call sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: Test waits for extension connection + tab.syncAll + warmup echo before the 50-call sequence
Audit log contains all 50 entries with correct tool name and ordering
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-network-auth.e2e.ts (fix audit log test)

Context:
The test fires 50 sequential echo calls. The first call fails with isError=true because the extension hasn't connected or the matching tab isn't ready. All subsequent calls may also fail.

The error at line 314:
```
expect(result.isError).toBe(false);
```

Fix: Check how the test sets up. It likely uses fixtures or manual setup but doesn't wait for the full pipeline. Add:
```typescript
await waitForExtensionConnected(mcpServer);
await waitForLog(mcpServer, 'tab.syncAll received', 15_000);
// Warmup call to verify dispatch works
const warmup = await mcpClient.callTool('e2e-test_echo', { message: 'warmup' });
expect(warmup.isError).toBe(false);
```

Before the 50-call loop.

Constraints:
- The test needs a matching tab open (test server + openTestAppTab or context.newPage to testServer.url)
- If the test uses base test (not fixtures), it must manually start test server and open matching tab
- Check the existing test setup carefully — it may be missing the test server entirely

userStories.title: Fix secret rotation test — echo fails because no matching tab ready
userStories.description: The secret rotation test calls echo before verifying the extension is connected and a matching tab is open. The echo returns isError because the e2e-test plugin has no ready tab. Fix by ensuring the full dispatch pipeline (extension + matching tab) is ready before the first echo call.
acceptanceCriteria: Test has a matching tab open (test server running + tab navigated to test URL)
Extension is connected and tab.syncAll received before first echo call
Warmup echo succeeds before the rotation chaos begins
Old client echo works before rotation
After rotation, old client gets 401, new client succeeds
npx playwright test e2e/stress-network-auth.e2e.ts --grep 'secret rotation' passes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-network-auth.e2e.ts (fix secret rotation test)

Context:
The error at line 391:
```
const echoResult = await oldClient.callTool('e2e-test_echo', { message: 'before-rotation' });
expect(echoResult.isError).toBe(false); // FAILS — isError is true
```

The echo fails because the e2e-test plugin has no matching tab in 'ready' state. The test sets up the MCP server and extension but doesn't open a tab matching the e2e-test plugin's URL pattern (http://localhost/*).

Fix: Add a test server and open a matching tab:
```typescript
const testServer = await startTestServer();
const appTab = await context.newPage();
await appTab.goto(testServer.url, { waitUntil: 'load' });
await waitForExtensionConnected(server);
await waitForLog(server, 'tab.syncAll received', 15_000);
```

Then the echo call will have a tab to dispatch to.

Constraints:
- Must add startTestServer to the setup
- Must clean up testServer in the finally block
- The matching tab must stay open throughout the rotation test
- After server restart (rotation), the extension reconnects but the tab is still there

userStories.title: Fix adapter file missing recovery test
userStories.description: The adapter file deletion + restoration test fails. Investigate the exact error and fix. Likely issues: the restored file isn't detected by the file watcher, or the hot reload doesn't re-inject into the new tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Deleting the adapter file does not crash the extension (health remains ok)
After restoring the file + hot reload, the new tab gets the adapter injected
Tool dispatch succeeds on the recovered tab
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-adapter-injection.e2e.ts (fix adapter file missing test)

Context:
This test deletes the adapter IIFE file, opens a new tab (expects injection to fail), restores the file, and expects recovery. The failure could be:
1. The file restoration path doesn't match what the server expects (different hash in filename)
2. The hot reload doesn't trigger adapter file re-creation
3. The new tab needs longer to reach ready state

Investigation steps for the worker:
1. Read the full test to understand the exact flow
2. Check the error message to identify which assertion fails
3. Look at the adapter file naming: the hash in the filename changes when content changes, so restoring the exact same content should produce the same filename
4. Verify the hot reload triggers adapter file writing (check server logs for 'Adapter files written')
5. Ensure the test waits long enough for re-injection after restore

Constraints:
- The adapter filename includes a content hash — restoring the same content should produce the same filename
- triggerHotReload may be needed after file restoration
- Use waitForLog and polling with generous timeouts for recovery
